tluaMetatableReplace.tlua(14,23): error TLUA2339: Property 'fromFirst' does not exist on type '{ fromSecond: string; own: boolean; }'.
tluaMetatableReplace.tlua(19,24): error TLUA2339: Property 'fromFirst' does not exist on type '{ own: boolean; }'.
tluaMetatableReplace.tlua(23,21): error TLUA2339: Property 'fromFirst' does not exist on type '{ own: boolean; }'.
tluaMetatableReplace.tlua(36,7): error TLUA2322: Type 'number' is not assignable to type 'boolean'.


==== tluaMetatableReplace.tlua (4 errors) ====
    // setmetatable *replaces* a metatable. A table that already had one keeps only what it
    // holds itself: the old __index does not answer any more, whether or not a new one takes
    // its place.
    
    local First = { fromFirst = 1 };
    local Second = { fromSecond = "s" };
    
    local obj = setmetatable({ own = true }, { __index = First });
    local f1: number = obj.fromFirst;
    
    local repaired = setmetatable(obj, { __index = Second });
    local s1: string = repaired.fromSecond;
    local own1: boolean = repaired.own;
    local gone = repaired.fromFirst;
                          ~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2339: Property 'fromFirst' does not exist on type '{ fromSecond: string; own: boolean; }'.
    
    // `setmetatable(t, nil)` is how a metatable is detached.
    local detached = setmetatable(obj, nil);
    local own2: boolean = detached.own;
    local gone2 = detached.fromFirst;
                           ~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2339: Property 'fromFirst' does not exist on type '{ own: boolean; }'.
    
    // A metatable with no __index detaches the fallback just as thoroughly.
    local other = setmetatable(obj, { __tostring = function(t: table): string return "t"; end });
    local gone3 = other.fromFirst;
                        ~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2339: Property 'fromFirst' does not exist on type '{ own: boolean; }'.
    
    // The metatable goes with it: nothing pairs these tables now.
    local dm = getmetatable(detached);
    
    // A read still goes through the *current* metatable.
    local rm = getmetatable(repaired);
    local back: string = rm.__index.fromSecond;
    
    // The statement form -- how Lua mutates a table in place -- narrows the variable, so its
    // fallback is visible afterward. (tluaMetatableStatement covers this in full.)
    local plain = { n = 1 };
    setmetatable(plain, { __index = First });
    local statementForm: boolean = plain.fromFirst;
          ~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA2322: Type 'number' is not assignable to type 'boolean'.
